subsection.heading: Establishment.—
subsection.chapeau: The President shall establish an independent, nonpartisan Commission within the executive branch to investigate any incident that results in the loss of—

paragraph.content: the International Space Station or its operational viability;

paragraph.chapeau: any other orbital or suborbital space vehicle carrying humans that is—

subparagraph.content: owned by the Federal Government; or
being used pursuant to a contract or Space Act Agreement with the Federal Government for carrying a government astronaut or a researcher funded by the Federal Government; or

paragraph.content: a crew member or passenger of any space vehicle described in this subsection.

subsection.heading: Deadline for Establishment.—
subsection.content: The President shall establish a Commission within 7 days after an incident specified in subsection (a).

paragraph.heading: Government astronaut.—
paragraph.content: The term “government astronaut” has the meaning given the term in section 50902.

paragraph.heading: Space act agreement.—
paragraph.content: The term “Space Act Agreement” means an agreement entered into by the Administration pursuant to its other transactions authority under section 20113(e).

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;

text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2017—Subsec. (a)(3). Pub. L. 115–10, § 838(1), amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “any other United States space vehicle carrying humans that is owned by the Federal Government or that is being used pursuant to a contract with the Federal Government; or”.
